Anything arbitrary, such as an arithmetical value or a fee.

examples.en: Benjamin Franklin's designation of "positive" and "negative" to different charges was arbitrary.

en: To move upward, to fly, to soar.

examples.en: He ascended to heaven upon a cloud.

en: To attribute a cause or characteristic to someone or something.

examples.en: One may ascribe these problems to the federal government; however, at this stage it is unclear what caused them.

en: A violent onset or attack with physical means, for example blows, weapons, etc.

examples.en: to make assault upon a man, a house, or a town

en: An assertion; a section of source code which tests whether an expected condition is true.

examples.en: He would often assert that there was life on other planets.

en: (grammar) In some Indo-European languages, a prefix e- (a- in Sanskrit) indicating a past tense of a verb.

examples.en: The money from renting out a spare room can augment a salary.

en: Of the same origin as claimed; genuine.

examples.en: The experts confirmed it was an authentic signature.

en: The art or science of making and flying aircraft.
The state or level of consciousness where sense data can be confirmed by an observer.

examples.en: I gradually passed from sleep to full awareness.

en: An area of low fertility and habitation, a desolate place.

examples.en: The pine barrens are a site lonely enough to suit any hermit.

en: In a fundamental, essential or basic manner

examples.en: They've added some fancy features, but it's basically still a car.

en: To endeavour to depress the price of, or prices in.

examples.en: to bear a railroad stock

en: In comfortable circumstances as regards property; forehanded.

examples.en: Will it be possible to have access to the room beforehand so that we can set up chairs?

en: The act of boycotting.
A gap or opening made by breaking or battering, as in a wall, fortification or levee / embankment; the space between the parts of a solid body rent by violence

examples.en: A clear breach is when the waves roll over the vessel without breaking. A clean breach is when everything on deck is swept away.

en: (often with "up") To make or become lively; to enliven; to animate.

examples.en: We took a brisk walk yesterday.

en: Size, specifically, volume.

examples.en: I understood the bulk of what you were saying, just one of two points I need to hear again.

en: A light blow or jolting collision.

examples.en: the bump of veneration; the bump of acquisitiveness

en: An administrative unit of government; office.

examples.en: a news bureau; a travel bureau; a service bureau; an employment bureau; the Citizens Advice Bureau

en: An act or instance of bursting.

examples.en: The bursts of the bombs could be heard miles away.

en: Able and efficient; having the ability needed for a specific task; having the disposition to do something; permitting or being susceptible to something.

examples.en: As everyone knew, he was capable of violence when roused.

en: A powdered substance produced by firing (calcining) calcium carbonate (limestone) and clay that develops strong cohesive properties when mixed with water. The main ingredient of concrete.

examples.en: the cement of our love

en: Unified; sticking together; making up a whole.
To occupy exactly the same space.

examples.en: The two squares coincide nicely.

en: The act of collapsing.

examples.en: She suffered a terrible collapse after slipping on the wet floor.

en: To begin, start.
Having sufficient skill, knowledge, ability, or qualifications.

examples.en: He is a competent skier and an expert snowboarder.

en: An act of compiling code.

examples.en: Samuel Johnson compiled one of the most influential dictionaries of the English language.

en: A protective substance that exists in the serum or other bodily fluid and is capable of killing microorganisms; complement.

examples.en: The complement of blue is orange.

en: To yield assent; to accord; to acquiesce, agree, consent; to adapt oneself, to conform.
A comprehensive school.

examples.en: When there are diametrically opposing views on a big issue that concerns millions of people, doing comprehensive research just makes sense.

en: To be made up of; to consist of (especially a comprehensive list of parts).

examples.en: The parts are comprised by the whole.

en: Something that is compulsory or required.

examples.en: The ten-dollar fee was compulsory.
examples.cn: 教育在某些国家是义务的。

en: To develop an idea; to form in the mind; to plan; to devise; to originate.

examples.en: Assisted procreation can help those trying to conceive.

en: That which affects one’s welfare or happiness. A matter of interest to someone. The adposition before the matter of interest is usually over, about or for.

examples.en: Judy's eyes filled with concern as she listened to the news report.
examples.cn: 环保是我们共同关切的事。

en: A solid mass formed by the coalescence of separate particles; a compound substance, a concretion.

examples.en: The road was made of concrete that had been poured in large slabs.

en: To concentrate toward the essence by making more close, compact, or dense, thereby decreasing size or volume.

examples.en: An abridged dictionary can be further condensed to pocket size.

en: To admit to the truth, particularly in the context of sins or crimes committed.

examples.en: I confess to spray-painting all over that mural!

en: (chiefly in the plural) A boundary or limit.
To stand or meet facing, especially in competition, hostility or defiance; to come face to face with

examples.en: We should confront him about the missing money.

en: Thorough, careful, or vigilant in one’s task performance.

examples.en: He was a thoughtful and conscientious worker.

en: A person who favors maintenance of the status quo.

examples.en: The curriculum committee at this university is extremely conservative.

en: (in the plural) Objects or facts that are coexistent, or in agreement with one another.

examples.en: He is very consistent in his political choices: economy good or bad, he always votes Labour!

en: A person or object that is not normal.
To end a law, system, institution, custom or practice.

examples.en: Slavery was abolished in the nineteenth century.

en: To be full to overflowing.

examples.en: Wild animals abound wherever man does not stake his claim.

en: An absurdity.
(usually capitalized) The garden where Plato taught.

examples.en: the military academy at West Point; a riding academy; the Academy of Music.; a music academy; a language academy

en: Having a secondary, supplementary or subordinate function by accompanying as a subordinate; aiding in a secondary way; being additional; being connected as an incident or subordinate to a principal; contributing or being contributory. Said of people and things, and, when of people, usually in a bad sense

examples.en: She was accessory to the riot.

en: Agreement; harmony; conformity; compliance.
The state of being accurate; being free from mistakes, this exemption arising from carefulness; exactness; correctness

examples.en: The value of testimony depends on its accuracy.

en: A sour substance.

examples.en: acid fruits or liquors

en: The act of acknowledging

examples.en: the acknowledgment of a favor

en: A state of being acquainted with a person; originally indicating friendship, intimacy, but now suggesting a slight knowledge less deep than that of friendship; acquaintanceship.

examples.en: I know of the man; but have no acquaintance with him.

en: To encourage development or induce increased activity; to stimulate.
To stick fast or cleave, as a glutinous substance does; to become joined or united.

examples.en: wax adhered to his finger

en: To be in contact or connection with.

examples.en: The living room and dining room adjoin each other.

en: To administer

examples.en: The job is to administrate the network.

en: A positive emotion including wonder and approbation; the regarding of another as being wonderful

examples.en: They looked at the landscape in admiration.
examples.cn: 我对她的毅力充满敬意。

en: The act of adopting.

examples.en: A Chinese baby girl was given away for adoption.

en: Being of advantage, beneficial.
Unfavorable; antagonistic in purpose or effect; hostile; actively opposing one's interests or wishes; contrary to one's welfare; acting against; working in an opposing direction.

examples.en: adverse criticism

en: A rod, wire, or other structure for receiving or transmitting radio, television signals etc.

examples.en: The seabirds put on an astonishing aerial display.

en: Somebody who is wealthy.

examples.en: They were affluent, but aspired to true wealth.

en: A temporally organized plan for matters to be attended to.
To make (an offence) worse or more severe; to increase in offensiveness or heinousness.

examples.en: He aggravated the story.

en: To disturb or excite; to perturb or stir up (a person).

examples.en: He was greatly agitated by the news.

en: A company that flies airplanes to transport people and goods.
A wing of a building, notably in a church separated from the nave proper by piers.

examples.en: Do you want to seat window or aisle?

en: An alarm.

examples.en: an airborne alert; ground alert

en: Any life form of extraterrestrial or extradimensional origin.

examples.en: alien subjects, enemies, property, or shores

en: To state under oath, to plead.

examples.en: The agency alleged my credit history had problems.

en: To make less severe, as a pain or difficulty.

examples.en: Alcohol is often a cheap tool to alleviate the stress of a hard day.

en: An abridgement or summary of a longer publication.

examples.en: He was wholly abstracted by other objects.

en: (usually a mass noun) Lodging in a dwelling or similar living quarters afforded to travellers in hotels or on cruise ships, or prisoners, etc.
The act of achieving or performing; a successful performance; accomplishment

examples.en: Finishing the game does not give you a 100% score until you have unlocked all of the achievements.

en: The act of administering; government of public affairs; the service rendered, or duties assumed, in conducting affairs; the conducting of any office or employment; direction.

examples.en: Successive US administrations have had similar Middle East policies.

en: A person who is in adolescence; someone who has reached puberty but is not yet an adult.
To promote or advantage.
The study of art or beauty.

examples.en: Her most recent works have this quirky, half-serious 90's teen culture-inspired aesthetic.

en: A mass, assemblage, or sum of particulars; something consisting of elements but considered as a whole.

examples.en: The aggregated soil.

en: To change the form or structure of.
Of or pertaining to analysis; resolving into elements or constituent parts

examples.en: an analytical experiment

en: A fair valuation or estimate of merit, worth, weight, etc.; recognition of excellence.

examples.en: We give to you this trophy as a token of our appreciation of all your years of service.
examples.cn: 他表达了对艺术的欣赏。

en: To make suitable; to suit.

examples.en: Let no man appropriate the use of a common benefit.

en: To estimate.

examples.en: NASA's Genesis spacecraft has on board an ion monitor to record the speed, density, temperature and approximate composition of the solar wind ions.

en: To determine, estimate or judge the value of; to evaluate

examples.en: He assessed the situation.

en: The act of assuming, or taking to or upon oneself; the act of taking up or adopting.

examples.en: His assumption of secretarial duties was timely.

en: Self-governing. Intelligent, sentient, self-aware, thinking, feeling, governing independently.
Inclination towards something; predisposition, partiality, prejudice, preference, predilection.

examples.en: Our prejudices bias our views.

en: To quote; to repeat, as a passage from a book, or the words of another.
An established law.
Someone or something living at the same time, or of roughly the same age as another.

examples.en: Cervantes was a contemporary of Shakespeare.

en: Arousing controversy—a debate or discussion of opposing opinions.
